[2018-01-26 04:02] supergreyman (Greyman): Nice little channel! 😄
[2018-01-26 04:08] supergreyman (Greyman): OK, serious question. How do you feel toward **sniping mechanic** like Apollo and Deadeye? In a typical Prismata game, your valuable unit (like Lucina) can't die until your defense breaks down. With the inclusion of Apollo that can bypass defense to kill, does it make Prismata too restrictive in how people play?
[2018-01-26 04:09] elyot: We plan on mostly designing snipe units with more restrictive targeting requirements than apollo, we generally avoid hard counters but that was kinda unavoidable
[2018-01-26 04:10] elyot: the thing is, in Prismata there is actually play around many hard counters, e.g. there are definitely games with both Apollo and Lucina where Lucina gets bought
[2018-01-26 04:10] elyot: So we actually think it's better for the game if a few of them exist, as long as it's not too many
[2018-01-26 04:11] elyot: like we want a lot of strategies to be viable in each game, but we also want a large variety of novel experiences and situations, and apollo is a source of a high number of them

messages.author: supergreyman
messages.content: Nice little channel! 😄

[2018-01-26 04:43] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, that is the ultimate goal for CCG-like games with tight card design. Apollo can create somewhat boring game if no player wants to take the risk of making powerful unit for fear of Apollo. But when one player does attempt to take the risk, the other player may react with Apollo which can create an interesting asymmetrical game. Giving Apollo a longer build time is a right direction IMO since it starts to feel like a tower defense game. 😛
[2018-01-26 04:43] supergreyman (Greyman): Somewhat like a mini-Zemora experience.

[2018-02-01 22:25] elyot: We do have some ideas of ways to get blue some other types of walls... they might have weaknesses like long buildtime or frontline
[2018-02-01 22:25] elyot: Fun design fact: most card games have unit abilities that are BUFFS... like taunt, divine shield, windfury, etc.
[2018-02-01 22:25] elyot: In prismata, most of the unit abilities are NERFS... lifespan, stamina, fragile, frontline
[2018-02-01 22:26] elyot: the reason is because Prismata is a game about being surprised and delighted by insights... so rather than give players units that look cool but actually suck, we give you units that look bad and let you discover that they're good

[2018-02-12 16:41] mrguy888: The issue with thermite core is that it is mainly good early to deny absorb. Early in the game the opponent is overdefended due to having their starter Engineers and their bigger absorber. Auride Core gets its value right away but Thermite Core has to wait many turns before those Pixies are relevant. Through the game Auride Core also gives you option value and gets really efficient when it lets you spend tech you otherwise ran too low on Drones to spend or against BP. Thermite Core just lets you kill Frontline stuff a bit faster.
